steps.title: Identify the initial value
steps.content: The input $x$ counts years after $2002$. At $x=0$, the estimated population is $3{,}500$, so the exponential model starts with $3{,}500$.

steps.title: Convert the growth rate
steps.content:
A $4\%$ yearly increase means each year is $104\%$ of the previous year.
$$104\%=1.04$$

steps.title: Write the model
steps.content: Use initial value times growth factor to the $x$ power. $$p(x)=3{,}500(1.04)^x$$ The answer is C.
